name: Government of India (Ministry of Commerce)
description: Indian government trade policy authority responsible for protecting domestic steel industry while maintaining WTO compliance and strategic partnerships.
objectives: Protect domestic steel industry employment (500,000+ direct and indirect jobs)
Maintain WTO compliance while pursuing retaliation options against unfair trade practices
Stabilize steel prices to prevent industrial cost inflation in downstream sectors
Diversify export markets to reduce dependency on any single trade partner
constraints: WTO commitments limit retaliatory tariff rates and require notification procedures
Fiscal deficit ceiling at 6% of GDP constrains subsidy scale and scope
DGTR investigation process has minimum 6-month duration by statute
Strategic partnership with US limits aggressive retaliation options
